titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ugo La Pietra Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 19, 2020 at 03:41

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seventeen series: I. Strutturazioni Tissurali, 1964-1971. II. Nodi Urbani, 1965-1975. III. Emergenze Urbane, 1966-1967. IV. Sistema Disequilibrante, 1966-1973. V.Casa Telematica MOMA New York, 1971-1972. VI. Il Commutatore, 1970-1971. VII. Gradi di Libertà: Recupero e Reinvenzione - Riappropriazione dell'Ambiente, 1969-1977. VIII. Gradi di Libertà: Itinerari Preferenziali, 1969-1974. IX. Informazione Alternativa - Tubi di Parigi, 1973. X. Global Tools, 1974-2013. XI. Riconversione Progettuale, 1918-2013. XII. Interno / Esterno: Abitare è Essere Ovunque a Casa Propria, 1979-1983. XIII. Interno / Esterno: Berlino Case Aperte, 1982-1984. XIV. Interno / Esterno: Casette, 1977-1978. XV. Film, 1972-1977. XVI. Letters and Documents, 1969-1984. XVII. Unidentified, undated.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ugo La Pietra is an Italian artist, architect, filmmaker, and designer, born in Bussi sul Tirino, Italy, in 1938. He has taught at the Politecnico di Milano and the Istituto Statale d'Arte di Monza. Pairing a sharp critique of modernist city planning with a playful and inventive impulse to experiment across multiple genres and formats, La Pietra's projects in the 1960s and 1970s often involved direct interventions in the urban landscape. His push for transgressive gestures of liberation, immersion, and reappropriation had much in common with radical groups such as Archizoom and Superstudio, with whom he collaborated in projects such as Global Tools, an experimental workshop and alternative school for rebellious young architects, artists, and designers in the early 1970s.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. The arrangement and titles of works are based on a list received with the collection. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, photographs, artwork, printed material and other papers by, to, or relating to Italian artist Ugo La Pietra. The collection documents La Pietra's creative process from the 1960s through the 1980s, exhibitions of his works, and his collaborations with other architects and artists such as Riccardo Dalisi, Gianni Pettena, and Franco Vaccari.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from L'Arengario Studio Bibliografico on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2014.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes photographs, artwork, posters, and catalogs documenting La Pietra's work in acrylic in the 1960s through early 1970s called Strutturazioni Tissurali, or "textual structures." It includes one acrylic object, "metracrilato lavorato a freddo e a caldo" (1971).

did.unittitle: 1.7 Object: Strutturazioni tissurali, metacrilato lavorato a freddo e a caldo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains sketches made in 1965 and 1966 as part of La Pietra's project "Nodi urbani," as well as a copy of his 1975 book Autoarchiterapia (number 25 of 500 autographed copies), which discusses the drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original designs, sketch and photograph of the model "Calotta ruotante."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes sketches, photographs, posters, and catalogs documenting works created by La Pietra related to his theory of the Sistema Disequilibrante, or "Unbalancing System." Includes documentation of his "Immersione" project.

did.unittitle: 4.19 Photograph: Al di là della pittura
4.20 Photograph: Al di là della pittura
4.21 Photograph: Al di là della pittura
4.22 Photograph: Al di là della pittura
4.26 Catalog: Il sistema disequilibrante, Galleria Toselli
4.29 Catalog: Il sistema disequilibrante, Galleria LP 220

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains photographs, collages and a photomontage/plan documenting La Pietra's projects Il Ciceronelettronico, La cellula ebitativa, and Il video comunicatore exhibited at MOMA in 1972.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes a photograph and two photomontages documenting La Pietra's project titled "Il Commutatore," a "comprehension model" affording alternate views of the streetscape.

did.unittitle: Gradi di Libertà: Recupero e Reinvenzione-Riappropriazione Dell'Ambiente

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, photomontages, collages, documents, and printed material documenting La Pietra's project concerning "degrees of freedom."

did.unittitle: 7.7 Article: Dalla Jugoslavia la riappropriazione dell'ambiente in INPIU'
7.10 Collage: I gradi di libertà: Recupero e reinvenzione
7.13 Book: La riappropriazione dell'ambiente. Interventi e analisi
7.14 Collage: Al di là della rete la città

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, photomontages, collages, and printed material documenting La Pietra's project concerning "degrees of freedom."

did.unittitle: 8.2 Photograph: Ugo La Pietra con Livio Marzot
8.3 File: Programma di ricerca La Pietra/Marzot
8.4 Catalog: Decodificazione dell'ambiente: sulla panchina, Galleria Blu
8.6 Frammento del menabò: Decodificazione dell'ambiente: sulla panchina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two photographs related to La Pietra's book "L'informazione alternativa [Tubi di Parigi]," and a copy of the book itself.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, photomontages, catalog and other papers documenting La Pietra's collaboration with other architects and designers on a series of experimental architecture and design workshops called Global Tools.

did.unittitle: 10.4 Photograph: Riuioni del gruppo Global Tooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes photomontages as well as a notebook, sketch, and book documenting La Pietra's project to redesign urban fixtures, distorting their use and making them home tools. Several items relate to "Poles and Chains."

did.unittitle: 11.7 Book: Attrezzaturen urbane per la collettività
Interno/Esterno: Abitare è Essere Ovunque a Casa Propria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketch, photograph, photomontage and book documenting La Pietra's "interior/exterior" project.

did.unittitle: 12.5 Book: Istruzioni per l'uso della città

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook, photograph and catalog documenting La Pietra's "interior/exterior" project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook, lithograph, and sketch documenting La Pietra's "interior/exterior" project.

did.unittitle: 14.3 Sketch: Interno esterno, china su cartoncino

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes the screenplay with the original montage of "Riappropriazione della città," with notes and sketches, as well as photographs, a poster and printed book of "La grande occasione."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains correspondence, photographs, writings, sketches, and other material documenting La Pietra's collaborations with other artists including Riccardo Dalisi, Gianni Pettena, and Franco Vaccari.

did.unittitle: 17.4 Typescript and and photograph related to G. Magro
17.5 Manuscript and 2 photographs: Perusini, "Descrizionone delle opere sull'inquinamento Sincron/Rimini
17.6 Manuscript and 11 photographs and 1 sketch by Richard Dalisi
17.7 Manuscript and 4 photographs: Franco Summa/Articolo per INPIU
17.8 Photomontage and sketch: Ugo La Pietra, Tracce
17.9 Draft: Gianni Pettena, article for INIPU 1973/1974
17.10 Letter and 2 photographs: Richard Dalisi a Ugo La Pietra, with article "Una Bianca striscia di carta
18.12 Photomontage and sketch: Ugo La Pietray, Fuoco e schiuma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of unidentified, undated material.
